_note: Round-13 follow-up #1: a `dispatch('ready', …)` call BEFORE the `const dispatch = createEventDispatcher()` declaration should NOT contribute 'ready' to the events surface. Pre-fix native called `find_untyped_dispatcher_local_names` as a pre-pass that collected ALL dispatcher locals globally; `find_dispatched_event_names` then scanned calls against that fully-populated set, so forward references registered wrongly. Upstream's TS walker visits the call BEFORE `setEventDispatcher` registers the binding (`processInstanceScriptContent.ts:271` — visit-then-recurse ordering on VariableStatement). Native now passes the dispatcher_locals tracking through `scan_statement_in_source_order` as a `&mut HashSet<String>` that grows incrementally — calls only resolve against locals seen so far. Inner here has `function fire() { dispatch('ready') }` before `const dispatch = …`; the call is visited first (in source order, even though the function body executes later), so 'ready' isn't added. Verify via `--emit-ts`: events surface is just `{[evt: string]: CustomEvent<any>}` — no specific 'ready' key. (Under non-strict mode the consumer-side typecheck doesn't fire either way; the divergence is at the analyze layer and visible in the emit shape.)
